mnemonics.title: Remember Traversal Orders
mnemonics.content: L R (LEFT BEFORE ROOT): INORDER = LNR (LEFT NODE RIGHT). R L (ROOT BEFORE LEFT): PREORDER = NLR (NODE LEFT RIGHT). L R R (LEFT RIGHT ROOT): POSTORDER = LRN (LEFT RIGHT NODE). TO REMEMBER: INORDER = IN (INSIDE), GO LEFT FIRST. PREORDER = PRE (FIRST), ROOT FIRST. POSTORDER = POST (LAST), ROOT LAST.

mnemonics.title: Traversal Applications
mnemonics.content: PREORDER FOR COPY (PREFIX): PRE = CREATE BEFORE CHILDREN. POSTORDER FOR DELETE (POST = AFTER): DELETE CHILDREN BEFORE PARENT. INORDER FOR SORT (IN = INSIDE SORTED): BST INORDER GIVES SORTED ORDER. LEVEL ORDER FOR SHORTEST PATH (LEVEL = LAYERS).

mnemonics.title: Space Complexity
mnemonics.content: RECURSIVE TRAVERSAL SPACE = HEIGHT (H). H = N IN SKEWED TREE (BAD). H = LOG N IN BALANCED TREE (GOOD). REMEMBER: STACK GROWS LIKE TREE BRANCHES.

acronyms.expansion: Depth First Search - explores as deep as possible before backtracking
acronyms.usage: Inorder, Preorder, and Postorder traversals are all DFS variants

acronyms.expansion: Breadth First Search - explores level by level
acronyms.usage: Level Order traversal is BFS for trees

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a person walking along the boundary of the tree from left to right, touching each node as they pass on the left side. The root sits between its left and right subtrees.
Think of painting the root first, then moving to paint the left subtree, then the right subtree. The root is always painted before its children.
Think of cutting leaves first, then branches, then the trunk. Children are always processed before their parent.
Imagine a flood filling the tree level by level from top to bottom, or ripples spreading outward from the root.
Think of a rope unwinding: each recursive call goes deeper (unwinding), then the stack winds back up as we return from each call.

analogies.analogy: Preorder is like checking each room then its contents then moving on. Inorder is like walking along the left wall, checking the corner, then moving through the center. Postorder is like checking contents of drawers before closing them.
Preorder: Mention ancestor before descendants. Postorder: Mention descendants before ancestor. Inorder: Mention family in birth order within each generation.

analogies.concept: Stack in Traversal as To-Do List
analogies.analogy: When doing inorder iteratively, the stack is like a to-do list: push left children to do later, process current node, then do right children. Like marking tasks pending.

analogies.concept: Queue in Level Order as Waiting Line
analogies.analogy: Level order uses a queue like a movie theater line: people (nodes) are served in order they arrive. When one person is served, their family (children) joins the end of the line.
Morris traversal creates temporary threads (shortcuts) to go back to previously visited nodes without a stack, like creating a bypass road to return without going back the long way.
